name: Swiss New Potatoes
recipeIngredient: salt and pepper and ground nutmeg
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub a shallow 2-quart baking dish generously with garlic.
2. Generously grease the dish with about half a tablespoon of butter.
3. In a bowl, combine lemon juice and water.
4. Peel potatoes if desired and cut into very thin slices; place in the lemon water until all are sliced.
5. Drain potatoes well and arrange about one-fourth of the slices in an even layer in the prepared dish.
6. Sprinkle lightly with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
7. Top with about one-fourth of the onions and cheese.
8. Repeat layers, ending with cheese.
9. Scald milk; slowly pour into egg yolk, beating constantly until blended.
10. Slowly pour over and around the potatoes.
11. Dot top with remaining butter.
12. Bake, uncovered, in a 350°F oven for about 1 1/2 hours or until potatoes are tender when pierced.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Savoy-style Swiss Potatoes (Gratin Savoyard): Prepare Swiss potatoes as directed, layering the potato slices with onions and cheese. Omit milk and egg and substitute 1 1/2 cups hot regular-strength beef broth; bake as directed.
